items.text: Retro: did the work buy back more time than its ceremony cost? (per-step verdict)
Decisions logged; repo-only boundary confirmed (nothing under plugin/, no engine code); handed off for operator accept + PR merge (lume close left to the operator).

dod.preamble: Closeout: verify the objective's done-when against evidence, record the retro, confirm the repo-only boundary, and hand off. Per lume's rule, accept and PR merge are the operator's; lume close is left to the operator to pair with that authority.

handback: Recorded retro.json: overall verdict worth-it; all six done-when clauses (a-f) assessed MET with evidence; per-stage verdicts net-positive; carry-forwards noted (session-start activation, chokepoint-not-sandbox, heuristic match, close left to operator). Confirmed the repo-only boundary: git diff touches only .claude/ and the repo-root CLAUDE.md plus this workstream's .lume state - nothing under plugin/ and no engine module. Handing off: the branch workstream/enforce-workstream-gate carries E1 (hook + settings) and E2 (CLAUDE.md); opening a PR for the operator to review and merge. Not self-accepting this closeout and not running lume close - those pair with the operator's accept/merge authority.
self_review: Holds: done-when is met on all clauses with concrete evidence, and the repo-only / no-engine boundary is verifiable from the diff. Consistency note for the operator: I drove the internal iteration gates (approve/start/handback/accept) for iterations 001-004 to build the change in one pass, but I am stopping this closeout at handback - I will not self-accept it, run lume close, or merge the PR, since accept and the externally-visible merge are the operator's gates (reinforced by this session's earlier correction). If you'd prefer I pause at every accept in future, say so.
